common.title: DevTools Hub
common.description: Enterprise-grade developer tools for modern workflows.
common.searchPlaceholder: Search tools by name or description...
common.subject: Subject

status.comingSoon: We are working hard to bring this feature to you soon!

hero.title: All-in-One Developer Hub
hero.subtitle: A suite of powerful, fast, and secure tools for modern developers. Everything runs in your browser.

clientSide.title: 100% Client-Side
clientSide.desc: Your data never leaves your browser. Secure and private by design.

fast.title: Blazing Fast
fast.desc: No server latency. Tools are instant and lightweight.

free.title: Always Free
free.desc: No tracking, no ads. Built for developers by developers.

aboutUs.title: About Us
aboutUs.p1: DevTools Hub was created with a single mission: to provide developers with a reliable, fast, and secure set of utilities for their everyday workflow. We understand the importance of privacy, which is why all our tools run 100% locally in your browser. No data is ever sent to a server.
aboutUs.p2: Whether you are decoding tokens, formatting messy code, or generating secure UUIDs, we've got you covered with a clean and distraction-free environment built entirely on modern web standards.

howItWorks.title: How it works
howItWorks.subtitle: Simple, secure, and privacy-first tools for your daily development tasks.

s1.title: Pick a tool
s1.desc: Select from our wide range of utilities.

s2.title: Input data
s2.desc: Paste your code, text, or files locally.

noMatches.title: No matches found
noMatches.desc: We couldn't find any tools matching "{search}"

notFound.title: Page Not Found
notFound.desc: The tool or page you are looking for does not exist or has been moved.
notFound.lost: Lost? Search for a tool on our {link}.

landing.title: Developer Utilities
landing.subtitle: Privacy-first, client-side tools for your daily development workflow.
landing.privacyDesc: All tools run entirely in your browser. Your data never leaves your machine.
landing.noToolsDesc: Try adjusting your search or category filters.

common.dropFile: Drop file here or click to upload

case-converter.title: Text Case Converter
case-converter.description: Convert text between camelCase, snake_case, PascalCase, and more.
case-converter.article: Easily switch between different naming conventions used in programming.

faqs.a0: Yes, kebab-case, snake_case, camelCase, and PascalCase are all included.

json-to-csv.title: JSON to CSV Converter
json-to-csv.description: Convert JSON data to CSV format with header detection.
json-to-csv.placeholder: Paste JSON array or object here...
json-to-csv.article: Convert structured JSON data into flat CSV format. Ideal for spreadsheet imports and data analysis.

faqs.a0: Yes, nested objects are flattened using dot notation.

json-formatter.title: JSON Formatter
json-formatter.description: Clean and format JSON data with ease.
json-formatter.article: JSON (JavaScript Object Notation) is a lightweight data-interchange format. Our JSON Formatter helps you beautify minified JSON, sort keys alphabetically, and even escape unicode characters for safe transmission.

faqs.q0: Is my JSON data sent to a server?
faqs.a0: No, all formatting is done entirely in your browser. Your data is 100% secure and private.
faqs.q1: Can I minify JSON here?
faqs.a1: Yes! Use the Minify toggle in the customization settings to remove all whitespace.

jwt-decoder.title: JWT Decoder
jwt-decoder.description: Decode and inspect JSON Web Tokens safely.
jwt-decoder.article: JSON Web Tokens (JWT) are an open, industry standard RFC 7519 method for representing claims securely between two parties. This decoder allows you to view the decoded header and payload of a JWT without needing to send it to a remote server.

faqs.a0: No, this tool only decodes the Base64Url encoded payload and header. Signature verification requires the secret key.
faqs.q1: Can I decode expired tokens?
faqs.a1: Yes, you can decode any JWT regardless of expiration. This tool shows the expiry status but doesn't prevent decoding expired tokens.

jwt-decoder.decoding_a_jwt: Decoding a JWT only shows its contents. It does not verify the signature. You should still verify tokens on your server.

regex-tester.title: Regex Tester
regex-tester.description: Test and debug regular expressions in real-time.
regex-tester.article: Regular expressions (Regex) are sequences of characters that define a search pattern. They are used for string matching and manipulation. This tester provides real-time feedback, supporting common flags like global (g), case-insensitive (i), and multiline (m).

faqs.a0: This tool uses the standard JavaScript (ECMAScript) regular expression engine.
faqs.q1: Can I perform replacements?
faqs.a1: Yes, use the 'Replace' tab to test regex replacements with capture group support (e.g., $1).

csv-to-json.title: CSV to JSON Converter
csv-to-json.description: Convert CSV data to JSON format with multi-row header support.
csv-to-json.article: Transform comma-separated values into JSON. Supports custom delimiters and header detection.

faqs.a0: Yes, you can specify the delimiter in the settings.

xml-formatter.title: XML Formatter
xml-formatter.description: Beautify and format XML data with syntax highlighting.
xml-formatter.article: Clean up messy XML code with proper indentation and node collapsing.

faqs.a0: Yes, it will highlight syntax errors if the XML is malformed.

yaml-validator.title: YAML Validator
yaml-validator.description: Validate and lint YAML data with line-level errors.
yaml-validator.article: Check your YAML files for syntax errors and structural validity. Supports YAML 1.2 standards.

toml-validator.title: TOML Validator
toml-validator.description: Check TOML syntax and preview as JSON.
toml-validator.article: Validate TOML configuration files and see how they map to JSON objects.

markdown-previewer.title: Markdown Previewer
markdown-previewer.description: Live preview GitHub-flavored Markdown with styling.
markdown-previewer.article: Write Markdown and see the rendered HTML instantly. Supports tables, task lists, and syntax highlighting.

faqs.a0: Yes, all standard GFM features like tables and task lists work perfectly.

word-counter.title: Word & Char Counter
word-counter.description: Count words, characters, lines, and reading time.
word-counter.article: Get detailed metrics for your writing, including readability scores and character counts with/without spaces.

faqs.a0: It's based on an average reading speed of 200-250 words per minute.

lorem-ipsum.title: Lorem Ipsum Generator
lorem-ipsum.description: Generate custom placeholder text for your designs.
lorem-ipsum.article: Generate standard Lorem Ipsum text by paragraphs, sentences, or words.

faqs.a0: Yes, you can specify the exact byte count for the output.

slug-generator.title: Slug Generator
slug-generator.description: Generate URL-safe slugs by stripping special characters.
slug-generator.article: Create SEO-friendly URLs by converting titles into lowercase, hyphenated strings.

faqs.a0: Yes, all non-alphanumeric characters except hyphens are stripped.

duplicate-remover.title: Duplicate Line Remover
duplicate-remover.description: Remove duplicate lines, sort, and trim whitespace.
duplicate-remover.article: Clean up lists by removing repeated entries and normalizing whitespace.

faqs.a0: You can toggle case-sensitivity in the settings.

whitespace-remover.title: Whitespace Remover
whitespace-remover.description: Trim all, leading, trailing, or extra whitespace.
whitespace-remover.article: Remove unnecessary spaces from your text to make it cleaner and more compact.

faqs.a0: Yes, the 'Strip All' option removes every single space character.

ascii-art.title: Text to ASCII Art
ascii-art.description: Convert text into stylized ASCII banner fonts.
ascii-art.article: Turn your text into large ASCII art banners for use in code comments or terminal headers.

faqs.a0: Yes, multiple classic ASCII fonts like 'Big', 'Slant', and 'Shadow' are available.

emoji-picker.title: Emoji Picker & Search
emoji-picker.description: Search and copy emojis for your projects.
emoji-picker.article: Find the perfect emoji quickly and copy it with a single click. Includes all the latest Emoji 15.0 additions.

faqs.a0: Yes, they are standard unicode emojis that work across all modern platforms.

text-sorter.title: Text Sorter
text-sorter.description: Sort text lines alphabetically, numerically, or shuffle them.
text-sorter.article: Organize lists of data quickly with various sorting algorithms.

faqs.a0: Yes, you can sort in both ascending and descending order.

line-number-adder.title: Line Number Adder
line-number-adder.description: Add custom line numbers, padding, and delimiters to text.
line-number-adder.article: Enumerate lines of text or code with customizable formatting.

faqs.q0: Can I start from a specific number?
faqs.a0: Yes, the starting number and increment are both configurable.

find-replace.title: Find & Replace Tool
find-replace.description: Advanced find and replace with regex and case support.
find-replace.article: Perform bulk text transformations using simple strings or powerful regular expressions.

faqs.a0: Yes, full JavaScript regular expression support is included.

string-reverse.title: String Reverse
string-reverse.description: Reverse characters or words with instant output.
string-reverse.article: Quickly flip your text backwards or reverse the order of words.

faqs.a0: Yes, there is a toggle to reverse words instead of individual characters.

base64-encoder.title: Base64 Encoder
base64-encoder.description: Encode and decode strings to/from Base64.
base64-encoder.placeholder: Enter text to encode or Base64 to decode...
base64-encoder.article: Base64 is an encoding scheme that represents binary data in an ASCII string format. It's commonly used when there is a need to encode data that needs to be stored and transferred over media that are designed to deal with textual data.

faqs.a0: URL-safe Base64 replaces the '+' and '/' characters with '-' and '_' respectively, making the encoded string safe for use in URLs and filenames.
faqs.q1: Can I encode files?
faqs.a1: Yes! This tool supports file upload for encoding. The file is read locally in your browser and converted to Base64 without any server upload.

timestamp-converter.title: Timestamp Converter
timestamp-converter.description: Convert between Unix timestamps and human dates.
timestamp-converter.article: A Unix timestamp is a way to track time as a running total of seconds. This tool helps you convert these numbers into readable date formats (ISO, Local, UTC) and calculate relative time differences.

faqs.a0: Yes, the tool automatically detects if the timestamp is in seconds or milliseconds and converts accordingly.
faqs.q1: How is local time calculated?
faqs.a1: Local time is calculated using your browser's current timezone settings.

api-tester.title: API Tester
api-tester.description: Test REST APIs with custom headers and bodies.
api-tester.responsePlaceholder: Send a request to see the response here
api-tester.article: An API (Application Programming Interface) tester allows developers to send HTTP requests to test RESTful APIs. You can customize headers, query parameters, and the request body to simulate complex API interactions.

faqs.a0: Yes, as long as your local API has CORS enabled to accept requests from this application.
faqs.q1: Are my requests saved?
faqs.a1: No, all request history and data are ephemeral and cleared when you refresh the page.

curl-generator.title: Curl Generator
curl-generator.description: Convert UI requests into executable Curl commands.
curl-generator.article: cURL is a command-line tool used to transfer data over various network protocols. This generator provides a user-friendly interface to construct complex cURL commands with headers, bodies, and methods.

faqs.q0: Can I generate multi-line cURL commands?
faqs.a0: Yes, toggle the 'Multiline Output' setting to format the command with backslashes for easier reading.
faqs.q1: Are headers automatically escaped?
faqs.a1: Yes, header values and JSON bodies are properly escaped to prevent shell injection and syntax errors.

sql-formatter.title: SQL Formatter
sql-formatter.description: Beautify and format complex SQL queries.
sql-formatter.article: SQL (Structured Query Language) is used to communicate with databases. Our formatter beautifies raw, minified, or messy SQL queries into readable, well-indented formats supporting multiple SQL dialects like MySQL, PostgreSQL, and T-SQL.

faqs.q0: Does formatting change my query logic?
faqs.a0: No, the formatter only alters whitespace and capitalization (if enabled) without modifying the underlying query logic.
faqs.q1: What dialects are supported?
faqs.a1: We support Standard SQL, MySQL, PostgreSQL, SQLite, T-SQL (SQL Server), and MariaDB.

custom.tip: Choose the correct dialect to ensure proper formatting of specialized syntax like backticks or schema prefixes.

color-palette-generator.title: Color Palette Generator
color-palette-generator.description: Generate and preview harmonious color schemes.
color-palette-generator.ruleDesc: Harmonies are calculated using HSL color space to ensure visual balance and accessibility.
color-palette-generator.article: Color palettes are essential for web design and branding. This tool uses HSL (Hue, Saturation, Lightness) color space mathematics to generate perfectly balanced harmonies such as analogous, triadic, and complementary schemes.

faqs.q0: How do I copy a color?
faqs.a0: Simply click on any color swatch in the preview or the list to copy its HEX code to your clipboard.
faqs.q1: What is an Analogous harmony?
faqs.a1: Analogous colors are groups of colors that are adjacent to each other on the color wheel, creating a serene and comfortable design.

uuid-generator.title: UUID Generator
uuid-generator.v4Desc: UUID v4 is the most commonly used version for random unique identifiers.
uuid-generator.article: A UUID (Universally Unique Identifier) is a 128-bit number used to identify information in computer systems. This tool generates UUID Version 4, which is based on random (or pseudo-random) numbers.

faqs.q0: Are the generated UUIDs truly unique?
faqs.a0: They use the browser's crypto API to generate highly secure, random UUIDs, making collisions practically impossible.
faqs.q1: Can I generate UUIDs in bulk?
faqs.a1: Yes, use the quantity slider to generate up to 20 UUIDs at once and copy them all with a single click.

image-to-base64.title: Image to Base64
image-to-base64.privacyNote: Images are processed entirely in your browser and never uploaded to any server.
image-to-base64.article: Converting an image to Base64 allows you to embed the image data directly into HTML, CSS, or JSON without needing external image files. This can reduce HTTP requests and improve initial page load times for small icons.

faqs.a0: Absolutely not. The conversion happens entirely within your browser using the FileReader API.
faqs.q1: What is the maximum image size?
faqs.a1: While there is no hard limit, we recommend keeping images under 5MB to avoid browser performance issues.

env-checker.title: ENV Checker
env-checker.description: Validate and check environment variable formats.
env-checker.securityDesc: ENV variables are parsed locally. This tool helps you visualize and clean your configuration before committing.
env-checker.textareaPlaceholder:
PASTE YOUR .ENV CONTENT HERE...
API_KEY=12345
DATABASE_URL=postgres://...
env-checker.article: Environment variables (.env files) store configuration settings and sensitive secrets. The ENV Checker helps you validate syntax, identify sensitive keys, and safely mask them before sharing or committing code.

faqs.a0: Yes, the tool is 100% client-side. No data is transmitted over the network.
faqs.q1: How does the tool identify secrets?
faqs.a1: It uses pattern matching to find common keywords like KEY, SECRET, PASSWORD, and TOKEN in the variable names.

code-diff-checker.title: Code Diff Checker
code-diff-checker.description: Compare two blocks of code to find differences.
code-diff-checker.article: A Code Diff Checker compares two text files or code snippets to highlight the differences. It is an essential tool for reviewing changes, debugging, and understanding modifications between code versions.

faqs.q0: What is the difference between Split and Unified views?
faqs.a0: Split view shows the old and new code side-by-side. Unified view shows all changes linearly in a single column.
faqs.q1: Can I ignore whitespace changes?
faqs.a1: Yes, simply toggle 'Ignore Whitespace' to focus only on structural code changes.

html-previewer.title: HTML Previewer
html-previewer.description: Render and preview HTML code snippets live.
html-previewer.layoutDesc: The preview runs in a sandboxed iframe for security. External scripts are disabled except for explicitly enabled libraries.
html-previewer.article: The HTML Previewer allows you to write and test HTML and CSS in real-time. It provides a sandboxed environment to visualize your code changes instantly, supporting modern features like Tailwind CSS via CDN.

faqs.a0: Yes, it uses an iframe with the 'sandbox' attribute to prevent malicious script execution.
faqs.q1: Can I use external CSS frameworks?
faqs.a1: Yes! You can toggle 'Include Tailwind' to automatically load Tailwind CSS via CDN.

css-gradient-generator.title: CSS Gradient Generator
css-gradient-generator.description: Design and generate CSS linear/radial gradients.
css-gradient-generator.tip: Drag the sliders to adjust color positions. You can have up to 5 color stops for complex gradients.
css-gradient-generator.article: CSS gradients let you display smooth transitions between two or more specified colors. This generator supports both linear and radial gradients, allowing you to visually design complex backgrounds and instantly copy the CSS code.

faqs.q0: How many color stops can I add?
faqs.a0: You can add up to 5 color stops to create intricate and detailed gradients.
faqs.q1: Can I adjust the angle of the gradient?
faqs.a1: Yes, for linear gradients, you can use the angle slider to rotate the gradient from 0 to 360 degrees.

url-encoder.title: URL Encoder/Decoder
url-encoder.description: Encode or decode URLs and parameters with full component mode toggles.
url-encoder.article: URL encoding (also known as percent-encoding) converts special characters in URLs into a format that can be transmitted over the Internet. This tool supports both full URL encoding (encodeURI) and component encoding (encodeURIComponent) for query parameters, ensuring your URLs are properly formatted and safe for web transmission.

faqs.q0: What's the difference between Component Mode and Full URL encoding?
faqs.a0: Component Mode (encodeURIComponent) encodes all special characters including / ? : @ & = +, making it ideal for query string parameters. Full URL mode (encodeURI) preserves these characters, suitable for encoding complete URLs.
faqs.q1: When should I use URL encoding?
faqs.a1: Use URL encoding when passing data in query strings, handling user input in URLs, or when special characters like spaces, &, or = appear in your URL parameters.

url-encoder.use_component_m: Use Component Mode (encodeURIComponent) for query string parameters. Turn it off to encode full URLs (encodeURI).

html-entity-encoder.title: HTML Entity Encoder
html-entity-encoder.description: Encode and decode HTML entities securely. Supports named and numeric entities.
html-entity-encoder.article: HTML entities are used to display reserved characters in HTML that would otherwise be interpreted as code. For example, < becomes < and > becomes >. This encoding is crucial for preventing XSS (Cross-Site Scripting) attacks when displaying user-generated content. This tool supports both named entities (&) and numeric entities (&).

faqs.q0: Why should I encode HTML entities?
faqs.a0: Encoding HTML entities prevents browsers from interpreting special characters as HTML tags or scripts, which is essential for security and preventing XSS attacks when displaying user input.
faqs.q1: What's the difference between named and numeric entities?
faqs.a1: Named entities like < are easier to read, while numeric entities like < work for any Unicode character. Named entities are limited to predefined characters, but numeric entities can represent any character.

html-entity-encoder.html_entity_enc: HTML entity encoding replaces characters like < and > with safe sequences to prevent XSS attacks.

jwt-builder.title: JWT Builder
jwt-builder.description: Build and sign JSON Web Tokens with custom payloads and algorithms.
jwt-builder.article: JSON Web Tokens (JWT) are a compact, URL-safe means of representing claims to be transferred between two parties. A JWT consists of three parts: Header (algorithm and token type), Payload (claims/data), and Signature (verification). This builder allows you to create custom JWTs with various signing algorithms (HS256, HS384, HS512) for testing and development purposes.

faqs.a0: This tool supports HMAC-based algorithms: HS256 (SHA-256), HS384 (SHA-384), and HS512 (SHA-512). These are symmetric algorithms that use the same secret key for signing and verification.
faqs.q1: Should I use this for production tokens?
faqs.a1: This tool is designed for development and testing. For production, always generate and sign JWTs on your secure backend server, never in the browser where secrets can be exposed.

jwt-builder.invalid_json_in: Invalid JSON in Header or Payload
jwt-builder.tokens_are_gene: Tokens are generated locally using the Web Crypto API. Your secret key never leaves your browser.

md5-generator.title: MD5 Generator
md5-generator.description: Generate MD5 hashes for strings and files instantly.
md5-generator.article: MD5 (Message Digest Algorithm 5) is a widely used cryptographic hash function that produces a 128-bit (32-character hexadecimal) hash value. While MD5 is fast and commonly used for checksums and file integrity verification, it is considered cryptographically broken and should NOT be used for password hashing or security-critical applications due to collision vulnerabilities.

faqs.a0: No! MD5 is cryptographically broken and vulnerable to collision attacks. Never use MD5 for password hashing. Use bcrypt, Argon2, or scrypt instead.
faqs.q1: What should I use MD5 for?
faqs.a1: MD5 is still acceptable for non-security purposes like file integrity checks, checksums, and detecting accidental data corruption. For security purposes, use SHA-256 or stronger algorithms.

md5-generator.enter_text_to_g: Enter text to generate MD5 hash...
md5-generator.md5_is_consider: MD5 is considered cryptographically broken and is prone to collision attacks. It should only be used for checksums and verifying data integrity, not for storing passwords.

sha-generator.title: SHA-1/256/512 Generator
sha-generator.description: Generate and compare SHA family hashes securely.
sha-generator.article: SHA (Secure Hash Algorithm) is a family of cryptographic hash functions designed by the NSA. SHA-1 produces a 160-bit hash but is now deprecated for security use. SHA-256 (256-bit) and SHA-512 (512-bit) are part of the SHA-2 family and remain cryptographically secure. These algorithms are widely used for digital signatures, certificate generation, and data integrity verification.

faqs.q0: Which SHA algorithm should I use?
faqs.a0: Use SHA-256 for most applications as it provides excellent security with good performance. SHA-512 offers higher security but is slower. Avoid SHA-1 for security purposes as it's deprecated due to collision vulnerabilities.
faqs.q1: Can I use SHA for password hashing?
faqs.a1: While SHA algorithms are secure, they're too fast for password hashing, making them vulnerable to brute-force attacks. Use specialized password hashing algorithms like bcrypt, Argon2, or PBKDF2 instead.

sha-generator.enter_text_to_g: Enter text to generate SHA hashes...
sha-generator.sha_256_and_sha: SHA-256 and SHA-512 are cryptographically secure and widely used for data integrity. SHA-1 is deprecated for security purposes but still used for legacy checksums.

bcrypt-generator.title: Bcrypt Generator
bcrypt-generator.description: Hash passwords using Bcrypt with configurable cost factors.
bcrypt-generator.article: Bcrypt is a password-hashing function designed by Niels Provos and David Mazières, based on the Blowfish cipher. It incorporates a salt to protect against rainbow table attacks and an adaptive cost factor that makes it resistant to brute-force attacks. The cost factor (work factor) determines how computationally expensive the hash is - each increment doubles the time required, making bcrypt future-proof as hardware improves.

faqs.q0: What cost factor should I use?
faqs.a0: A cost factor of 10-12 is recommended for most applications, providing a good balance between security and performance. Higher values (13-14) offer more security but may impact user experience during login.
faqs.q1: Why does bcrypt take so long?
faqs.a1: Bcrypt is intentionally slow to prevent brute-force attacks. The cost factor determines the number of iterations - higher values exponentially increase computation time, making password cracking impractical.

bcrypt-generator.higher_cost_fac: Higher cost factors take exponentially longer to compute. Values > 12 may freeze your browser.
bcrypt-generator.bcrypt_is_a_pas: Bcrypt is a password-hashing function designed to be slow and compute-intensive. This protects against brute-force attacks.

argon2-generator.title: Argon2 Hash Generator
argon2-generator.description: Generate modern Argon2 password hashes with tunable memory and iterations.
argon2-generator.article: Argon2 is the winner of the 2015 Password Hashing Competition and is considered the most secure password hashing algorithm available today. It comes in three variants: Argon2d (data-dependent, resistant to GPU attacks), Argon2i (data-independent, resistant to side-channel attacks), and Argon2id (hybrid, recommended for most use cases). Argon2 is designed to resist both GPU and ASIC attacks by requiring significant memory resources.

faqs.q0: Why is Argon2 better than bcrypt?
faqs.a0: Argon2 provides better resistance to GPU and ASIC attacks by utilizing memory-hard functions. It also offers more tunable parameters (memory cost, time cost, parallelism) allowing better customization for your security needs.
faqs.q1: What parameters should I use?
faqs.a1: For web applications, recommended settings are: memory cost 65536 KB (64 MB), time cost 3 iterations, and parallelism 4. Adjust based on your server's capabilities and security requirements.

argon2-generator.argon2_is_the_w: Argon2 is the winner of the Password Hashing Competition. It is designed to be highly resistant to GPU cracking attacks by utilizing heavy memory constraints.

hmac-generator.title: HMAC Generator
hmac-generator.description: Generate Hash-based Message Authentication Codes with various algorithms.
hmac-generator.article: HMAC (Hash-based Message Authentication Code) is a mechanism for message authentication using cryptographic hash functions combined with a secret key. Unlike simple hashing, HMAC provides both data integrity and authenticity verification. It's widely used in API authentication (like AWS Signature), JWT signing, and secure communication protocols. HMAC can use various hash functions (MD5, SHA-1, SHA-256, SHA-512) with SHA-256 being the most commonly recommended.

faqs.q0: What's the difference between HMAC and regular hashing?
faqs.a0: Regular hashing only verifies data integrity. HMAC adds authentication by incorporating a secret key, ensuring both that the data hasn't been tampered with AND that it came from someone who knows the secret key.
faqs.q1: When should I use HMAC?
faqs.a1: Use HMAC for API request signing, webhook verification, secure cookie signing, and any scenario where you need to verify both the integrity and authenticity of a message. It's commonly used in OAuth, JWT, and AWS API authentication.

hmac-generator.hmac_uses_a_cry: HMAC uses a cryptographic hash function in combination with a secret key to verify both data integrity and authenticity.

morse-converter.title: Morse Code Converter
morse-converter.description: Translate text to Morse code and vice versa.
morse-converter.article: Morse code is a method of encoding text characters as sequences of dots (·) and dashes (−), developed by Samuel Morse in the 1830s. Each letter, number, and punctuation mark has a unique pattern. Morse code was historically used for long-distance communication via telegraph and radio, and remains relevant today in aviation, amateur radio, and assistive technology for people with disabilities.

faqs.q0: How do I read Morse code?
faqs.a0: Dots (·) represent short signals and dashes (−) represent long signals. Letters are separated by spaces, and words by longer spaces. For example, SOS is ··· −−− ··· (three dots, three dashes, three dots).
faqs.q1: Can I hear the Morse code?
faqs.a1: Yes! This tool includes an audio playback feature that converts the Morse code into audible beeps, with short beeps for dots and longer beeps for dashes, just like traditional telegraph communication.

morse-converter.info: Convert plain text into Morse code dots and dashes, or translate Morse back into text. You can also play the Morse code as audio.

base-converter.title: Base Converter
base-converter.description: Convert between Binary, Octal, Decimal, and Hexadecimal.
base-converter.article: Number base conversion is fundamental in computer science and programming. Binary (base-2) uses only 0 and 1, representing how computers store data. Octal (base-8) uses digits 0-7 and was historically used in computing. Decimal (base-10) is our everyday number system. Hexadecimal (base-16) uses 0-9 and A-F, commonly used for memory addresses, color codes, and compact binary representation. This tool uses JavaScript's BigInt for handling extremely large numbers without precision loss.

faqs.a0: Hexadecimal provides a compact way to represent binary data. Each hex digit represents exactly 4 binary bits, making it easier to read and write than long binary strings. It's commonly used for memory addresses, color codes (#FF5733), and debugging.
faqs.q1: What's the largest number I can convert?
faqs.a1: This tool uses JavaScript's BigInt, which can handle arbitrarily large integers limited only by available memory. You can safely convert numbers with hundreds or thousands of digits.

base-converter.invalid_charact: Invalid character for the selected base
base-converter.this_converter: This converter uses BigInt, allowing you to convert extremely large numbers safely without losing precision.

caesar-cipher.title: Caesar Cipher
caesar-cipher.description: Encrypt and decrypt text using the Caesar cipher with adjustable shifts.
caesar-cipher.article: The Caesar cipher is one of the oldest and simplest encryption techniques, named after Julius Caesar who used it for military communications. It works by shifting each letter in the alphabet by a fixed number of positions. For example, with a shift of 3, A becomes D, B becomes E, and so on. While historically significant, Caesar cipher is not secure by modern standards and should only be used for educational purposes or simple obfuscation.

faqs.a0: No, Caesar cipher is extremely insecure by modern standards. With only 25 possible shifts, it can be easily broken by trying all possibilities (brute force) or using frequency analysis. Never use it for protecting sensitive information.
faqs.q1: What is the brute force feature?
faqs.a1: The brute force feature shows all 25 possible decryptions by trying every shift value from 1 to 25. This demonstrates how easy it is to break Caesar cipher encryption without knowing the key.

caesar-cipher.caesar_cipher_i: Caesar Cipher is a substitution cipher where each letter is shifted a certain number of places down the alphabet.

rot13-encoder.title: ROT13 Encoder
rot13-encoder.description: Quickly encode and decode text using ROT13 cipher.
rot13-encoder.article: ROT13 ("rotate by 13 places") is a special case of the Caesar cipher with a shift of 13. Because the English alphabet has 26 letters, applying ROT13 twice returns the original text, making encoding and decoding the same operation. ROT13 is commonly used on internet forums to hide spoilers, punchlines, or potentially offensive content. It provides no security and is purely for obfuscation.

faqs.a0: ROT13 is its own inverse - applying it twice returns the original text. This is because shifting by 13 in a 26-letter alphabet means encoding and decoding use the exact same operation, making it simple and symmetric.
faqs.q1: Where is ROT13 used?
faqs.a1: ROT13 is commonly used in online forums, newsgroups, and comment sections to hide spoilers, puzzle solutions, or potentially offensive jokes. It's not encryption - it's just a way to prevent accidental reading.

rot13-encoder.enter_text_to_e: Enter text to encode/decode with ROT13...
rot13-encoder.rot13_is_a_simp: ROT13 is a simple letter substitution cipher that replaces a letter with the 13th letter after it in the alphabet. Because there are 26 letters in the alphabet, encoding and decoding use the same operation.

punycode-converter.title: Punycode Converter
punycode-converter.description: Convert Internationalized Domain Names (IDN) to Punycode.
punycode-converter.article: Punycode is a encoding syntax used to represent Unicode characters in domain names using only ASCII characters. Since DNS (Domain Name System) traditionally only supports ASCII, Punycode allows internationalized domain names (IDN) with characters from languages like Arabic, Chinese, Cyrillic, and emoji. Punycode domains start with 'xn--' prefix. For example, 'münchen.de' becomes 'xn--mnchen-3ya.de'.

faqs.q0: Why do I see 'xn--' in some domains?
faqs.a0: The 'xn--' prefix indicates a Punycode-encoded domain name containing non-ASCII characters. It's how browsers and DNS servers handle internationalized domain names while maintaining compatibility with ASCII-only systems.
faqs.q1: Are Punycode domains safe?
faqs.a1: Punycode itself is safe, but be cautious of homograph attacks where similar-looking characters from different alphabets (like Cyrillic 'а' vs Latin 'a') are used to create deceptive domain names that look legitimate.

punycode-converter.info: Punycode converts Unicode characters into an ASCII-compatible format required for internationalized domain names (IDNs).

unicode-inspector.title: Unicode Inspector
unicode-inspector.description: Inspect Unicode characters, code points, and categories.
unicode-inspector.article: Unicode is a universal character encoding standard that assigns a unique number (code point) to every character across all writing systems, symbols, and emoji. Code points are typically written in hexadecimal with a U+ prefix (e.g., U+0041 for 'A'). This inspector helps developers debug text encoding issues, understand character composition, and identify invisible or special characters that may cause problems in code or data.

faqs.a0: A code point is Unicode's unique identifier for each character, written as U+ followed by hexadecimal digits. For example, U+0041 is 'A', U+1F600 is 😀. Code points range from U+0000 to U+10FFFF.
faqs.q1: Why do some characters show multiple code points?
faqs.a1: Some characters are composed of multiple code points, like emoji with skin tone modifiers or accented letters using combining diacritical marks. For example, 👨‍👩‍👧 (family) is actually multiple code points joined together.

unicode-inspector.info: Inspect string characters to reveal their exact Unicode hex code points and decimal representations.

html-color-to-hex.title: HTML Color to HEX
html-color-to-hex.description: Convert named HTML colors to their HEX equivalents.
html-color-to-hex.article: HTML supports 140 named colors (like 'red', 'blue', 'coral') that can be used in CSS and HTML. However, hexadecimal color codes (#RRGGBB) are more precise and universally supported. This tool converts between named colors, RGB, HSL, and HEX formats, helping you maintain consistency across your designs. HEX colors are preferred in production code for their compactness and universal browser support.

faqs.q0: What color formats can I convert?
faqs.a0: This tool accepts HTML named colors (e.g., 'tomato'), RGB format (rgb(255, 99, 71)), HSL format (hsl(9, 100%, 64%)), and HEX codes. It converts all formats to standardized HEX output.
faqs.q1: Why use HEX instead of named colors?
faqs.a1: HEX codes offer precise color control with 16.7 million possible colors, while named colors are limited to 140 options. HEX is also more compact, universally supported, and easier to manipulate programmatically.

html-color-to-hex.info: Convert standard HTML named colors, RGB, or HSL strings directly into standardized hexadecimal color codes.

aes-encryptor.title: AES Encryptor
aes-encryptor.description: Encrypt and decrypt data using AES with CBC/GCM modes.
aes-encryptor.article: AES (Advanced Encryption Standard) is a symmetric encryption algorithm adopted by the U.S. government and used worldwide for securing sensitive data. AES-256 uses a 256-bit key, providing extremely strong encryption. This tool implements AES in CBC (Cipher Block Chaining) mode with PBKDF2 key derivation, converting your passphrase into a secure encryption key. The output is Base64-encoded for easy storage and transmission.

faqs.a0: AES-256 is considered military-grade encryption and is virtually unbreakable with current technology. It would take billions of years to crack using brute force. However, security depends on using a strong, unique passphrase.
faqs.q1: Can I decrypt data encrypted elsewhere?
faqs.a1: This tool uses OpenSSL-compatible format (Salted__ prefix). You can decrypt the output using OpenSSL command-line tools or other compatible libraries, as long as you use the same passphrase.

aes-encryptor.decryption_fail: Decryption failed. Incorrect passphrase or invalid ciphertext.
aes-encryptor.this_acts_as_th: This acts as the master password to encrypt/decrypt.
aes-encryptor.advanced_encryp: Advanced Encryption Standard (AES) is a symmetric encryption algorithm. The ciphertext is encoded in Base64 and prefixed with the OpenSSL salt marker (U2Fsd...).
aes-encryptor.enter_aes_ciphe: Enter AES ciphertext (starting with U2Fsd...) to decrypt...

rsa-key-generator.title: RSA Key Generator
rsa-key-generator.description: Generate RSA public and private key pairs securely.
rsa-key-generator.article: RSA (Rivest-Shamir-Adleman) is an asymmetric cryptographic algorithm that uses a pair of keys: a public key for encryption and a private key for decryption. RSA is fundamental to modern internet security, used in HTTPS, SSH, digital signatures, and secure email. This tool generates RSA key pairs in PEM format, compatible with OpenSSL and most cryptographic libraries. Key sizes of 2048 or 4096 bits are recommended for security.

faqs.q0: What key size should I use?
faqs.a0: 2048-bit keys are currently considered secure for most applications and offer good performance. 4096-bit keys provide extra security margin but are slower. Avoid 1024-bit keys as they're no longer considered secure.
faqs.q1: How do I use these keys?
faqs.a1: The public key can be shared freely and is used to encrypt data or verify signatures. The private key must be kept secret and is used to decrypt data or create signatures. Never share your private key!

rsa-key-generator.keys_are_genera: Keys are generated entirely within your browser memory. They are never transmitted over the internet.

qrcode-decoder.title: QR Code Decoder
qrcode-decoder.description: Decode QR code images and extract text content.
qrcode-decoder.article: QR (Quick Response) codes are two-dimensional barcodes that can store various types of data including URLs, text, contact information, and more. Invented in 1994 by Denso Wave for automotive industry, QR codes are now ubiquitous in marketing, payments, and authentication. This decoder uses computer vision algorithms to detect and extract data from QR code images entirely in your browser, ensuring your data never leaves your device.

faqs.a0: This tool supports common image formats including PNG, JPEG, GIF, and WebP. For best results, ensure the QR code is clearly visible, well-lit, and not distorted or damaged.
faqs.q1: Why can't it read my QR code?
faqs.a1: Common issues include low image quality, poor lighting, QR code damage, or extreme angles. Try taking a clearer photo with better lighting, ensuring the entire QR code is visible and in focus.

qrcode-decoder.upload_desc: Select an image containing a QR Code to decode locally.
qrcode-decoder.no_qr_found: No QR code found in the image.
qrcode-decoder.info: QR Code Decoder reads and extracts text from QR images entirely within your browser for maximum privacy.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.description: Your privacy is our top priority. Learn how we handle your data.
privacy.intro: At DevTools Hub, we take your privacy seriously. Our platform is designed with a privacy-first approach.

section1.title: 100% Client-Side Processing
section1.content: All tools on this platform run entirely in your browser. We do not use servers to process your data, meaning your code, JSON, tokens, and images never leave your machine.

section2.title: No Data Storage
section2.content: We do not store your inputs, outputs, or any personal information. Once you close the tab or refresh the page, all session data is cleared from your browser's memory.

section3.title: Analytics and Cookies
section3.content: We may use minimal, privacy-respecting analytics to understand how our tools are used. No personal identifiers are collected, and we do not use tracking cookies for advertising.

github.title: GitHub Repository
github.description: DevTools Hub is an open-source project. Contribute on GitHub.
github.intro: We believe in the power of open-source. DevTools Hub is built by developers, for developers.

contribute.title: Contribute to the Project
contribute.content: Found a bug? Have a feature request? Feel free to open an issue or submit a pull request. We welcome contributions from the community.

license.title: MIT License
license.content: The project is licensed under the MIT License, which means you are free to use, modify, and distribute the code.

changelog.description: Stay updated with the latest features and improvements.
